headline: New York City Offers Free Childcare Through 'Parents' Night Out'
meta_description: New York City Parks Department will provide free childcare for 500 children through a new 'Parents' Night Out' program across the city.
summary_short: New York City is launching a free childcare program that will serve 500 children across the city, giving parents more breathing room.
body:
The New York City Parks Department has launched a new "Parents' Night Out" program that will provide free childcare for 500 children across the city. The initiative recognizes that parents—especially those with limited resources—face constant pressure to balance work, family, and self-care.

By removing the cost barrier, the program makes it possible for families to access the support they need. Parents gain the chance to rest, attend to their own needs, or simply have uninterrupted time together, while children benefit from supervised, enriching activities. It's a straightforward but powerful acknowledgment that helping families thrive strengthens entire communities.
